volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 4.7.810 - Section 5.2.870)

sections.label: Section 4.7.810
sections.translation: What are we to do now ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.810b
sections.original: Quin redimus ? Iam haec tibi aderit supplicans
sections.translation: Why , go back again : she ’ ll soon be with you , of her own accord , to entreat forgiveness .

sections.label: Section 4.7.811b
sections.translation: Do you think so ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.811c
sections.translation: Certainly , yes . I know the disposition of women : when you will , they won ’ t ; when you won ’ t , they set their hearts upon you of their own inclination .

sections.label: Section 4.7.812b
sections.translation: You judge right .

sections.label: Section 4.7.813
sections.translation: Shall I dismiss the army then ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.813b
sections.translation: Whenever you like .

sections.label: Section 4.7.813c
sections.translation: Sanga , as befits gallant soldiers , [*] take care in your turn to remember your homes and hearths .

sections.label: Section 4.7.815
sections.translation: My thoughts have been for some time among the sauce-pans .

sections.label: Section 4.7.815b
sections.translation: You are a worthy fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.7.815c
sections.translation: You follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 5.1.816
sections.translation: What ! do you persist , hussy , in talking ambiguously to me ? I do know ; I don ’ t know ; he has gone off ; I have heard ; I wasn ’ t there . Don ’ t you mean to tell me plainly , whatever it is ? The girl in tears , with her garments torn , is mute ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.820
sections.original: Eunuchus abiit ; quamobrem ? quid factum est ? taces ?
sections.translation: the Eunuch is off : for what reason ? What has happened ? Won ’ t you speak ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.821
sections.original: Quid tibi ego dicam misera ? Illum eunuchum negant
sections.translation: Wretch that I am , what am I to say to you ? They declare that he was not a Eunuch .

sections.label: Section 5.1.822b
sections.translation: What was he then ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.822c
sections.translation: That Chaerea .

sections.label: Section 5.1.823
sections.translation: What Chaerea ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.823b
sections.translation: That stripling , the brother of Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 5.1.824
sections.translation: What ’ s that you say , you hag ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.824b
sections.translation: And I am satisfied of it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.825
sections.original: Quid is obsecro ad nos ? quamobrem adductus est ?
sections.translation: Pray , what business had he at my house ? What brought him there ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.825b
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; unless , as I suppose , he was in love with Pamphila .

sections.label: Section 5.1.826b
sections.translation: Alas ! to my confusion , unhappy woman that I am , I ’ m undone , if what you tell me is true . Is it about this that the girl is crying ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.828b
sections.translation: I believe so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.828c
sections.translation: How say you , you arch-jade ? Did I not warn you about this very thing , when I was going away from here ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.830
sections.original: Quid facerem ? ita ut tu iusti soli credita est .
sections.translation: What could I do ? Just as you ordered , she was intrusted to his care only .

sections.label: Section 5.1.831
sections.translation: Hussy , I ’ ve been intrusting the sheep to the wolf . I ’ m quite ashamed to have been imposed upon in this way . What sort of man was he ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.833
sections.original: Hera mea tace ; tace obsecro ; salvae sumus :
sections.translation: Hush ! hush ! mistress , pray ; we are all right . Here we have the very man .

sections.label: Section 5.1.834b
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.834c
sections.translation: Why there , to the left .

sections.label: Section 5.1.835
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.835b
sections.translation: I see .

sections.label: Section 5.1.835c
sections.translation: Order him to be seized as quickly as possible .

sections.label: Section 5.1.836
sections.translation: What can we do to him , simpleton ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.836b
sections.translation: What do to him , do you ask ? Pray , do look at him ; if his face doesn ’ t seem an impudent one .

sections.label: Section 5.1.838
sections.original: Videtur : non est ? tum quae eius confidentia est !
sections.translation: Besides , what effrontery he has .

sections.label: Section 5.2.839
sections.original: Apud Antiphonem uterque , mater et pater ,
sections.translation: At Antipho ’ s , [*] both of them , father and mother ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.840
sections.original: Quasi dedita opera domi erant , ut nullo modo
sections.translation: just as if on purpose , were at home , so that I couldn ’ t any way get in , but that they must have seen me . In the mean time , while I was standing before the door , a certain acquaintance of mine was coming full upon me . When I espied him , I took to my heels as fast as I could down a narrow unfrequented alley ; thence again

sections.label: Section 5.2.845
sections.original: In aliud , inde in aliud : ita miserrimus
sections.translation: to another , and thence to another ; thus have I been most dreadfully harassed with running about , that no one might recognize me . But isn ’ t this Thais that I see ? It is she . I ’ m at a stand . What shall I do ? But what need I care ? What can she do to me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.849
sections.original: Adeamus . Bone vir Dore salve . Die mihi ,
sections.translation: Let ’ s accost him . ( To CHAEREA .) Good Mister Dorus , welcome ; tell me ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.850
sections.translation: have you been running away ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.850b
sections.translation: Madam , I did so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850c
sections.translation: Are you quite pleased with it ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.851
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 5.2.851b
sections.translation: Do you fancy that you ’ ll get off with impunity ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.851c
sections.translation: Forgive this one fault ; if I ’ m ever guilty of another , then kill me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.853
sections.translation: Were you in fear of my severity ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.853b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 5.2.853c
sections.translation: No ? What then ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.854
sections.original: Hanc metui ne me criminaretur tibi .
sections.translation: I was afraid of her , lest she might be accusing me to you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.855
sections.translation: What had you done ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.855b
sections.translation: A mere trifle .

sections.label: Section 5.2.855c
sections.translation: Come now , a trifle , you impudent fellow . Does this appear a trifle to you , to ravish a virgin , a citizen ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.857b
sections.translation: I took her for my fellow-servant .

sections.label: Section 5.2.858
sections.original: Conservam ? Vix me contineo quin involem in
sections.translation: Fellow-servant ? I can hardly restrain myself from flying at his hair . A miscreant ! Even of his own free will he comes to make fun of us .

sections.label: Section 5.2.860
sections.translation: Won ’ t you begone from here , you mad woman ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.860b
sections.translation: Why so ? Really , I do believe I should be something in this hang-dog ’ s debt , if I were to do so ; especially as he owns that he is your servant .

sections.label: Section 5.2.863
sections.original: Missa haec faciamus . Non te lignum , Chaerea ,
sections.translation: We ’ ll pass that by . Chaerea , you have behaved unworthily of yourself ; for if I am deserving in the highest degree of this affront ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.865
sections.original: Sum maxime , at tu indignius qui faceres tamen .
sections.translation: still it is unbecoming of you to be guilty of it . And , upon my faith , I do not know what method now to adopt about this girl : you have so confounded all my plans , that I can not possibly return her to her friends in such a manner as is befitting and as I had intended ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.870
sections.original: Ut solidum parerem hoc mihi beneficium , Chaerea .
sections.translation: in order that , by this means , I might , Chaerea , do a real service to myself .
